document_metadata.title: Where to find the best Halloween decorations around Richmond
document_metadata.first_words: Richmond’s neighborhoods come alive each October with the Halloween spirit, ranging from downright frightening to kid-friendly whimsy. ,There are animatronics, zombie takeovers, parades and plenty of 12-foot skeletons standing guard. ,Halloween is near, so let the boos begin. , ,In North Chesterfield at 10533 Ashburn Road, Julie Watson and Bill McNeill, both 58, are ready to collect some screams. Every inch of the yard is part of the performance. There’s fog, homemade tombstones, Annabelle hanging in the trees, and scare actors hiding in plain sight. What started as small yard decorations has turned into a full-scale production.

content_metadata.document_title_txt: Where to find the best Halloween decorations around Richmond

chunks.content_original:
,

This year, they’re extending their display to include a Halloween tunnel in the driveway and another in the backyard.

,

“That one’s going to be a little bit scarier,” she said. “We’re going to hire some actors, volunteer actors, to do some little jump scares. We have all these ambitious ideas, and trying to make them come to real life is a challenge but we enjoy it, myself and my husband. As long as we’ve got good knees and a good back.”

,

The couple starts prepping early.

,

“We start staging in the backyard in June to make sure they’re working, if they need any touch-ups or repairs,” she said. “We had one of the 12-foot skeletons fall over last year, so his ribs were broken. So right now, we’re doing some plastic surgery on him.”

,

Her two youngest children still help out, offering ideas and dressing up every year. “Because you live in my house, you got to dress up,” she said.
chunks.content:
,

This year, they’re extending their display to include a Halloween tunnel in the driveway and another in the backyard.

,

“That one’s going to be a little bit scarier,” she said. “We’re going to hire some actors, volunteer actors, to do some little jump scares. We have all these ambitious ideas, and trying to make them come to real life is a challenge but we enjoy it, myself and my husband. As long as we’ve got good knees and a good back.”

,

The couple starts prepping early.

,

“We start staging in the backyard in June to make sure they’re working, if they need any touch-ups or repairs,” she said. “We had one of the 12-foot skeletons fall over last year, so his ribs were broken. So right now, we’re doing some plastic surgery on him.”

,

Her two youngest children still help out, offering ideas and dressing up every year. “Because you live in my house, you got to dress up,” she said.

chunks.content_original:
,

Head to **2105 Maplewood Road** for the Dark Woods of Lakeside or 9420 Gayton Road for skeletons, lights, witches, cauldrons and giant spiders.

,

If you’re looking for something more fanciful, head to **3433 Floyd Ave.** in the Museum District for the Harry Potter House where Kyle and Sarah Rasmussen have turned their house into a page from a book.

,

“Halloween has always been a big thing in my family. My parents both enjoyed Halloween and decorated a lot when I was a kid,” Kyle said. “I’ve done Harry Potter the last six years that we’ve lived in Richmond. Each year, we do the next book.”

,

This year is “Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince.”

,

“Every year there’s some old favorites, like the werewolves and the spiders and the dragon on top of the carport,” she said. “But the whole front of the house is completely different.”

,

The house has become a local favorite and a social media hit.

,

,

,

,

,

“Every year there’s some old favorites, like the werewolves and the spiders and the dragon on top of the carport,” she said. “But the whole front of the house is completely different.”

The house has become a local favorite and a social media hit.

chunks.content_original:
,

The house has become a local favorite and a social media hit.

,

“We usually have a lot of people stopping by,” he said. “Towards Halloween, we’ll probably get like 50 to 100 people during the day that will walk by it.”

,

At night, Kyle says it’s too many to count.

,

Preparation starts early. “I used to wait till the first week of October, but since we have two small kids now, I start the second week of September just because it takes a little bit longer. And that time only gives me so much time to put stuff up.”

,

In Henrico, **[The Haunt in Chamberlayne Farms](https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=61558301124456)** is back with a haunted walk-through experience at 5914 Chamberlayne Road.

,

Oregon Hill has many Halloween decorated houses in preparation for the **[All the Saints Theater Company’s 20th annual Halloween Parade](https://www.facebook.com/allthesaintstheatercompany)** on Oct. 31.

,

chunks.content_original:
,

In North Chesterfield at **10533 Ashburn Road,** Julie Watson and Bill McNeill, both 58, are ready to collect some screams. Every inch of the yard is part of the performance. There’s fog, homemade tombstones, Annabelle hanging in the trees, and scare actors hiding in plain sight. What started as small yard decorations has turned into a full-scale production.

,

Watson says Halloween has always been a favorite holiday. “It comes from a very young age. I love dressing up,” she said. “You get dressed up and you get candy. What kid does not want to do that?”

,

That love of Halloween rolled over into making costumes for her children, doing hands-on DIY projects, and entering contests.

,

Her top costumes include a Rubik’s cube and a Chinese food to-go box for her son. One year, he won three costume contests helping Julie secure $3,000 off of his braces.
chunks.content:
,

In North Chesterfield at **10533 Ashburn Road,** Julie Watson and Bill McNeill, both 58, are ready to collect some screams. Every inch of the yard is part of the performance. There’s fog, homemade tombstones, Annabelle hanging in the trees, and scare actors hiding in plain sight. What started as small yard decorations has turned into a full-scale production.

,

Watson says Halloween has always been a favorite holiday. “It comes from a very young age. I love dressing up,” she said. “You get dressed up and you get candy. What kid does not want to do that?”

,

That love of Halloween rolled over into making costumes for her children, doing hands-on DIY projects, and entering contests.

,

Her top costumes include a Rubik’s cube and a Chinese food to-go box for her son. One year, he won three costume contests helping Julie secure $3,000 off of his braces.

Every year, the Harry Potter house at 3433 Floyd Ave. gets decorated to the theme of a different Harry Potter book.

,

**More must-see Halloween houses around Richmond**The app Fright maps has an up-to-date list of decorated Halloween houses in the area like the haunted house at **4913 Sadler Glen Place in Glen Allen** with plenty of pumpkins, tombstones and even Jack Skellington.

chunks.content_original:
,

“We like putting a smile on the kids’ faces,” she added. “Our house is off Huguenot Road between St. Edward’s Church and Huguenot Church, so we get a lot of traffic. When schools let out, there’s a line of cars just rolling slowly, looking at our display.”

, [![](https://bloximages.newyork1.vip.townnews.com/richmond.com/content/tncms/assets/v3/editorial/e/e9/ee9f5caa-989a-476e-b125-d0750cf9bfff/68f8ed7e638c4.image.jpg?resize=100%2C67)](https://richmond.com/image_ee9f5caa-989a-476e-b125-d0750cf9bfff.html)

A giant animatronic werewolf is part of the halloween decorations at 10533 Ashburn Road in North Chesterfield.

chunks.content_original:
, [![](https://bloximages.newyork1.vip.townnews.com/richmond.com/content/tncms/assets/v3/editorial/a/f2/af22ec18-2ba4-4fb2-b6da-3375e85df917/68f8edb2b810b.image.jpg?resize=100%2C67)](https://richmond.com/image_af22ec18-2ba4-4fb2-b6da-3375e85df917.html)

## [10xx25-rtd-met-halloween](https://richmond.com/image_af22ec18-2ba4-4fb2-b6da-3375e85df917.html)

“We like putting a smile on the kids’ faces,” Julie Watson said. “When schools let out, there’s a line of cars just rolling slowly, looking at our display.”

,
,

“For myself, it was the pumpkin scarecrow. I sat in front of the casket, slumped over in a chair, and then I would tap on the casket, and I’d say, ‘Boo.’ They thought I was a prop,” she said. “We started off little, and we got good feedback from the neighborhood kids. And we’re always like, ‘What can we do better or what can we do more of?’ And it has just grown over the years to what we have now.”

,

The display has grown so much she had to buy a second shed just to store it.

, 

## [10 must-see Halloween houses in Richmond](https://richmond.com/life-entertainment/local/article_d2527709-fe7b-4d55-a846-db440bc7f8eb.html)

If you think Richmond is creative the rest of the year, just wait until Halloween. From giant skeletons and fog-filled graveyards to entire streets transformed overnight, neighborhoods across the city go all out to get spooky.

,

“I even have a casket that’s an actual Hollywood prop. Just the casket, no dead bodies,” she joked.

,

Richmond’s neighborhoods come alive each October with the Halloween spirit, ranging from downright frightening to kid-friendly whimsy.

,

There are animatronics, zombie takeovers, parades and plenty of 12-foot skeletons standing guard.

,

Halloween is near, so let the boos begin.

markdown_content:
[

Richmond’s neighborhoods come alive each October with the Halloween spirit, ranging from downright frightening to kid-friendly whimsy.

,

There are animatronics, zombie takeovers, parades and plenty of 12-foot skeletons standing guard.

,

Halloween is near, so let the boos begin.

, [![](https://bloximages.newyork1.vip.townnews.com/richmond.com/content/tncms/assets/v3/editorial/4/f1/4f120014-1aa5-4d34-b830-4b3a29662960/68f8ed92b4bf9.image.jpg?resize=100%2C67)](https://richmond.com/image_4f120014-1aa5-4d34-b830-4b3a29662960.html)

## [10xx25-rtd-met-halloween](https://richmond.com/image_4f120014-1aa5-4d34-b830-4b3a29662960.html)

Julie Watson and Bill McNeill have turned their home at 10533 Ashburn Road in North Chesterfield into a spooky Halloween spectacle.

,

In North Chesterfield at **10533 Ashburn Road,** Julie Watson and Bill McNeill, both 58, are ready to collect some screams. Every inch of the yard is part of the performance. There’s fog, homemade tombstones, Annabelle hanging in the trees, and scare actors hiding in plain sight. What started as small yard decorations has turned into a full-scale production.

,

Watson says Halloween has always been a favorite holiday. “It comes from a very young age. I love dressing up,” she said. “You get dressed up and you get candy. What kid does not want to do that?”

,

That love of Halloween rolled over into making costumes for her children, doing hands-on DIY projects, and entering contests.

,

Her top costumes include a Rubik’s cube and a Chinese food to-go box for her son. One year, he won three costume contests helping Julie secure $3,000 off of his braces.

, [![](https://bloximages.newyork1.vip.townnews.com/richmond.com/content/tncms/assets/v3/editorial/a/f2/af22ec18-2ba4-4fb2-b6da-3375e85df917/68f8edb2b810b.image.jpg?resize=100%2C67)](https://richmond.com/image_af22ec18-2ba4-4fb2-b6da-3375e85df917.html)

## [10xx25-rtd-met-halloween](https://richmond.com/image_af22ec18-2ba4-4fb2-b6da-3375e85df917.html)

“We like putting a smile on the kids’ faces,” Julie Watson said. “When schools let out, there’s a line of cars just rolling slowly, looking at our display.”

,

“For myself, it was the pumpkin scarecrow. I sat in front of the casket, slumped over in a chair, and then I would tap on the casket, and I’d say, ‘Boo.’ They thought I was a prop,” she said. “We started off little, and we got good feedback from the neighborhood kids. And we’re always like, ‘What can we do better or what can we do more of?’ And it has just grown over the years to what we have now.”

,

The display has grown so much she had to buy a second shed just to store it.

, 

## [10 must-see Halloween houses in Richmond](https://richmond.com/life-entertainment/local/article_d2527709-fe7b-4d55-a846-db440bc7f8eb.html)

If you think Richmond is creative the rest of the year, just wait until Halloween. From giant skeletons and fog-filled graveyards to entire streets transformed overnight, neighborhoods across the city go all out to get spooky.

,

“I even have a casket that’s an actual Hollywood prop. Just the casket, no dead bodies,” she joked.

,

“We like putting a smile on the kids’ faces,” she added. “Our house is off Huguenot Road between St. Edward’s Church and Huguenot Church, so we get a lot of traffic. When schools let out, there’s a line of cars just rolling slowly, looking at our display.”

, [![](https://bloximages.newyork1.vip.townnews.com/richmond.com/content/tncms/assets/v3/editorial/e/e9/ee9f5caa-989a-476e-b125-d0750cf9bfff/68f8ed7e638c4.image.jpg?resize=100%2C67)](https://richmond.com/image_ee9f5caa-989a-476e-b125-d0750cf9bfff.html)

## [10xx25-rtd-met-halloween](https://richmond.com/image_ee9f5caa-989a-476e-b125-d0750cf9bfff.html)

A giant animatronic werewolf is part of the halloween decorations at 10533 Ashburn Road in North Chesterfield.

,

This year, they’re extending their display to include a Halloween tunnel in the driveway and another in the backyard.

,

“That one’s going to be a little bit scarier,” she said. “We’re going to hire some actors, volunteer actors, to do some little jump scares. We have all these ambitious ideas, and trying to make them come to real life is a challenge but we enjoy it, myself and my husband. As long as we’ve got good knees and a good back.”

,

The couple starts prepping early.

,

“We start staging in the backyard in June to make sure they’re working, if they need any touch-ups or repairs,” she said. “We had one of the 12-foot skeletons fall over last year, so his ribs were broken. So right now, we’re doing some plastic surgery on him.”

,

Her two youngest children still help out, offering ideas and dressing up every year. “Because you live in my house, you got to dress up,” she said.

, [![](https://bloximages.newyork1.vip.townnews.com/richmond.com/content/tncms/assets/v3/editorial/1/30/130bc762-ea2c-4d10-82dd-f4f3d9f0f935/68f7036113997.image.jpg?resize=100%2C75)](https://richmond.com/image_130bc762-ea2c-4d10-82dd-f4f3d9f0f935.html)

## [Harry Potter house on Floyd Ave.](https://richmond.com/image_130bc762-ea2c-4d10-82dd-f4f3d9f0f935.html)

Every year, the Harry Potter house at 3433 Floyd Ave. gets decorated to the theme of a different Harry Potter book.

,

**More must-see Halloween houses around Richmond**The app Fright maps has an up-to-date list of decorated Halloween houses in the area like the haunted house at **4913 Sadler Glen Place in Glen Allen** with plenty of pumpkins, tombstones and even Jack Skellington.

,

Head to **2105 Maplewood Road** for the Dark Woods of Lakeside or 9420 Gayton Road for skeletons, lights, witches, cauldrons and giant spiders.

,

If you’re looking for something more fanciful, head to **3433 Floyd Ave.** in the Museum District for the Harry Potter House where Kyle and Sarah Rasmussen have turned their house into a page from a book.

,

“Halloween has always been a big thing in my family. My parents both enjoyed Halloween and decorated a lot when I was a kid,” Kyle said. “I’ve done Harry Potter the last six years that we’ve lived in Richmond. Each year, we do the next book.”

,

This year is “Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince.”

,

“Every year there’s some old favorites, like the werewolves and the spiders and the dragon on top of the carport,” she said. “But the whole front of the house is completely different.”

,

The house has become a local favorite and a social media hit.

,

“We usually have a lot of people stopping by,” he said. “Towards Halloween, we’ll probably get like 50 to 100 people during the day that will walk by it.”

,

At night, Kyle says it’s too many to count.

,

Preparation starts early. “I used to wait till the first week of October, but since we have two small kids now, I start the second week of September just because it takes a little bit longer. And that time only gives me so much time to put stuff up.”

,

In Henrico, **[The Haunt in Chamberlayne Farms](https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=61558301124456)** is back with a haunted walk-through experience at 5914 Chamberlayne Road.

,

Oregon Hill has many Halloween decorated houses in preparation for the **[All the Saints Theater Company’s 20th annual Halloween Parade](https://www.facebook.com/allthesaintstheatercompany)** on Oct. 31.

,

And of course, **Hanover Avenue, the Bellevue Neighborhood,** and **Church Hill** are always hot spots for trick-or-treating.

, [![](https://bloximages.newyork1.vip.townnews.com/richmond.com/content/tncms/assets/v3/editorial/e/71/e717f310-97e2-11ef-a20c-9f3f0e00a188/672418738c7be.preview.jpg?resize=100%2C67)](https://richmond.com/life-entertainment/local/collection_e717f310-97e2-11ef-a20c-9f3f0e00a188.html)

## [Trick-or-treating in Richmond on Halloween 2024](https://richmond.com/life-entertainment/local/collection_e717f310-97e2-11ef-a20c-9f3f0e00a188.html)

]
